property_constraints.constraint: Minimum contiguous clear area of 1,500 square feet required for Standard installations and 2,000 square feet for Premium installations
property_constraints.reason: Manifold spacing and fog dispersal physics require minimum area for safe and effective operation. Undersized installations produce pooling, oversaturation, and accelerated surface degradation.

property_constraints.constraint: Property grade must not exceed 6 percent slope within the installation zone
property_constraints.reason: Ground fog is gravity-dependent. Slopes exceeding 6 percent cause fog to channel and pool at the low point rather than distributing evenly. This creates both an aesthetic failure and a slip hazard on hard surfaces.
property_constraints.disposition: Hard disqualifier — no exceptions. Grade can sometimes be corrected through landscaping prior to installation, which Nimbus can advise on but does not perform.

property_constraints.constraint: Installation zone must demonstrate positive drainage away from all structures. Any area that holds standing water for more than 30 minutes after a one-inch rainfall event is disqualified.
property_constraints.reason: Fog fluid is water-soluble and adds moisture to the ground surface. Poor drainage combined with fog operation creates saturated soil conditions that damage turf, promote fungal growth, and can undermine foundation-adjacent soil stability.
property_constraints.disposition: Hard disqualifier. Drainage correction by a licensed landscaper prior to installation can resolve this — Nimbus will re-survey after correction.

property_constraints.constraint: No fog system installation within 10 feet of any pool edge, spa, hot tub, or open water feature
property_constraints.reason: Ground-level fog obscures the surface boundary of water features, creating a drowning hazard. This setback is non-negotiable regardless of pool fencing or cover status.
property_constraints.disposition: Hard disqualifier — no exceptions, no variance

property_constraints.constraint: No fog system installation within 15 feet of any HVAC outdoor intake vent, return vent, or fresh air exchanger
property_constraints.reason: Glycol-based fog drawn into HVAC intake can coat evaporator coils, reduce system efficiency, and in rare cases trigger nuisance alarms on indoor air quality monitors. The 15-foot setback eliminates this under all wind conditions up to 12 mph.
property_constraints.disposition: Hard disqualifier. In some cases, HVAC intake relocation by the homeowner's HVAC contractor can resolve this.

property_constraints.constraint: Fog systems cannot be installed on or over solid concrete, solid pavers without joints, composite decking, or artificial turf
property_constraints.reason: Non-permeable and synthetic surfaces trap moisture from fog operation, creating slip hazards and accelerated surface degradation. Artificial turf specifically traps glycol residue that degrades the backing material.
property_constraints.disposition: Hard disqualifier for the specific surface. Mixed-surface properties may qualify if the permeable area meets minimum size requirements independently.

property_constraints.constraint: Systems are not rated for operation when ambient temperature exceeds 95°F
property_constraints.reason: Above 95°F, the chiller unit cannot maintain the fluid temperature differential required for ground-hugging fog behavior. The fog rises rather than staying low, producing a visible haze effect rather than the intended ground layer. Extended operation above 95°F also accelerates compressor wear.
property_constraints.disposition: Operational limitation — the system includes automatic high-temperature shutoff at 95°F. This is a built-in safety feature, not a defect. In the DFW climate, this typically affects fewer than 25 operating days per year.

property_constraints.constraint: Nimbus does not install fog systems on properties located in designated wildfire risk zones as mapped by the Texas A&M Forest Service
property_constraints.reason: Ground fog can obscure evacuation paths and interfere with firefighter visibility during wildfire events. Additionally, the glycol fluid reservoir constitutes a minor accelerant risk in extreme fire conditions.
property_constraints.disposition: Hard disqualifier. The site assessment includes wildfire zone verification.

property_constraints.constraint: Properties with active outdoor pest control contracts using broadcast pyrethroid sprays must suspend broadcast application within the installation zone for a minimum of 14 days before installation and switch to targeted bait-station pest control going forward
property_constraints.reason: Broadcast pyrethroids leave surface residue that becomes aerosolized by fog dispersal, creating an inhalation exposure risk for people and pets in the fog zone.
property_constraints.disposition: Conditional — resolvable by switching pest control methods. Nimbus provides documentation for the homeowner's pest control provider.

property_constraints.constraint: HOA-governed properties require written HOA approval before Nimbus will schedule installation
property_constraints.reason: Fog systems alter the visible appearance of the property and may affect neighboring properties. Several DFW-area HOAs have specific provisions regarding outdoor lighting, water features, and landscape effects that may apply. Nimbus has a standard HOA information packet but cannot guarantee approval.
property_constraints.disposition: Conditional — Nimbus will not begin work without documentation of HOA approval. The homeowner is responsible for obtaining approval.

service_area_constraints.constraint: Service area limited to the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex: Denton, Collin, Tarrant, Dallas, and western Kaufman counties
service_area_constraints.reason: Fog systems require calibration and maintenance visits that are not economically viable beyond the DFW service radius.
service_area_constraints.zip_codes_served: All zip codes within Denton, Collin, Tarrant, and Dallas counties. Western Kaufman County zip codes: 75114, 75142, 75126, 75152, 75160, 75182.
service_area_constraints.disposition: Hard boundary — no installations outside the defined service area

service_area_constraints.constraint: Commercial event installations require the venue to be within 45 minutes drive time of Argyle, TX during non-peak traffic
service_area_constraints.reason: Temporary event installations require same-day setup and teardown logistics that are not viable at extended distances.
service_area_constraints.disposition: Hard boundary for single-event rentals. Multi-day or seasonal commercial installations may be evaluated on a case-by-case basis.

scope_of_practice_boundaries.statement: Nimbus Ground Effects does NOT perform electrical work. All dedicated circuits required for fog system operation must be installed by a licensed electrician before Nimbus begins installation.
scope_of_practice_boundaries.reason: Texas law requires a licensed electrician for 240V circuit installation. Nimbus coordinates timing but does not perform, supervise, or warranty any electrical work.

scope_of_practice_boundaries.statement: Nimbus Ground Effects does NOT perform grading, drainage correction, or landscape construction. If the site assessment identifies drainage or grade issues, the homeowner must engage a licensed landscaper to correct them before Nimbus will proceed.
scope_of_practice_boundaries.reason: Drainage and grading are prerequisite conditions, not part of the fog system installation scope.

scope_of_practice_boundaries.statement: Nimbus Ground Effects does NOT install or service indoor fog systems of any kind.
scope_of_practice_boundaries.reason: Indoor fog systems require fire suppression system coordination, HVAC integration engineering, and building code compliance that are outside Nimbus's scope and licensing. Nimbus is exclusively an outdoor ground-fog company.

scope_of_practice_boundaries.statement: Nimbus does not service, repair, or maintain fog systems installed by other companies.
scope_of_practice_boundaries.reason: Nimbus cannot warranty or guarantee the performance of systems built to unknown specifications with unknown components. Homeowners with non-Nimbus systems who want Nimbus service must commission a full replacement installation.
